title: Savory Coated Turkish Lamb Dolsot Kebap
description: A resinous, crumbly Turkish dish featuring casabe and coconut cream, perfect for post-workout. This dish is a lacto-vegetarian-friendly take on the traditional Adana kebap, served in a dolsot stone bowl.

ingredients.name: Coconut cream
ingredients.notes: cracked to separate cream and coconut oil

instructions: In a bowl, mix together the flour, breadcrumbs, red pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper.
Cut the lamb shoulder into thin strips and season with salt and black pepper.
Coat the lamb strips with the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Heat the olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat and cook the coated lamb strips until browned, about 3 minutes per side.
Transfer the cooked lamb to a baking dish and bake in the preheated oven for 10 minutes.
While the lamb is cooking, heat the coconut cream in a small saucepan over low heat until warmed through, stirring occasionally.
To assemble the dolsot kebap, place a portion of the cooked lamb in the bottom of a dolsot stone bowl, followed by a spoonful of the warmed coconut cream, and finally a sprinkle of casabe.
Cook the lamb in two batches if necessary to prevent overcrowding the pan.
